title: Math — Probability
description: PSAT 8/9 probability and conditional probability questions (two-way tables, complements, and proportion-to-count conversions).

questions.prompt: A grove has 6 rows of birch trees and 5 rows of maple trees. Each row of birch trees has 8 trees 20 feet or taller and 6 trees shorter than 20 feet. Each row of maple trees has 9 trees 20 feet or taller and 7 trees shorter than 20 feet. A tree from one of these rows will be selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a maple tree, given that the tree is 20 feet or taller?
questions.explanation: Given the tree is 20+ ft, restrict to 20+ ft trees only. Birch 20+ ft: \(6\times 8=48\). Maple 20+ ft: \(5\times 9=45\). Total 20+ ft: \(48+45=93\). Conditional probability \(=\frac{45}{93}=\frac{15}{31}\).
choiceExplanations: Uses the wrong total (mixes in <20 ft trees or all trees).
Not a conditional probability on the 20+ ft subset.
Uses an incorrect total for the 20+ ft subset.

questions.prompt:
Employees at an electric company recorded all calls last Monday and noted whether the caller asked for repairs and whether the caller asked about a bill. The table shows the results:

- Asked about a bill: 48 asked for repairs, 623 did not ask for repairs (total 671)
- Did not ask about a bill: 130 asked for repairs, 90 did not ask for repairs (total 220)

If a caller who asked about a bill is selected at random, which is closest to the probability that the caller also asked for repairs?
questions.explanation: Conditional probability: \(P(\text{repairs} \mid \text{asked about bill})=\frac{48}{671}\approx 0.0715\), which is closest to 0.07.
choiceExplanations: Too small; would come from using the wrong denominator or a different conditional.
Correct: \(48/671\approx 0.07\).
This is closer to \(48/220\) or another wrong conditional setup.
This is closer to \(178/671\) or using totals incorrectly.

questions.prompt: The coat color and eye color for 48 Himalayan kittens are summarized in a table. Cream-tortoiseshell: 16 deep blue, 16 light brown (32 total). Chocolate: 12 deep blue, 4 light brown (16 total). What fraction of the chocolate-colored kittens has deep blue eyes?
questions.explanation: Restrict to chocolate-colored kittens only (total 16). Of those, 12 have deep blue eyes. Fraction \(=\frac{12}{16}=\frac{3}{4}\).
choiceExplanations: Uses all kittens (48) instead of only chocolate-colored kittens.
Uses the deep-blue total (28) instead of the chocolate total (16).
Uses the cream-tortoiseshell row instead of the chocolate row.
Correct: chocolate deep blue / chocolate total \(=12/16\).

questions.prompt: In a bag, there are 7 red, 4 white, 33 blue, and 33 yellow cubes. If one cube is selected at random, what is the probability of selecting a cube that is neither blue nor yellow?
questions.explanation: “Neither blue nor yellow” means the cube is red or white. Favorable: \(7+4=11\). Total: \(7+4+33+33=77\). Probability \(=\frac{11}{77}=\frac{1}{7}\).
choiceExplanations: Represents selecting blue or yellow (the opposite event).
Not consistent with \(11/77\); likely denominator error.
Not consistent with \(11/77\); likely total-count error.

questions.prompt: A table shows 14 cars listed for sale by type and price. Nonhybrid: 5 priced at no more than $25,000 and 3 priced greater than $25,000 (8 total). Hybrid: 2 priced at no more than $25,000 and 4 priced greater than $25,000 (6 total). If one car is selected at random, what is the probability it is a hybrid priced at no more than $25,000?
questions.explanation: The event is “hybrid AND priced \(\le 25{,}000\)”: there are 2 such cars out of 14 total. Probability \(=\frac{2}{14}=\frac{1}{7}\).
choiceExplanations: This would be \(4/14\) or another incorrect cell/total mix.
This would be \(2/6\) (conditional on being hybrid).
This would be \(8/14\) or another incorrect total.

questions.prompt: At a conference, there are 275 attendees. Each attendee is assigned to group A, B, or C. If an attendee is selected at random, the probability the attendee is assigned to group A is 0.44 and the probability the attendee is assigned to group B is 0.24. How many attendees are assigned to group C?

At a movie theater, there are 350 customers located in theater A, B, or C. If a customer is selected at random, the probability the customer is in theater A is 0.48, and the probability the customer is in theater B is 0.24. How many customers are located in theater C?
choiceExplanations: Looks like \(0.08\times 350\) (wrong leftover probability).
Arithmetic slip after subtracting.
Equals the count in B, not C.

questions.prompt:
The table shows the distribution of U.S. states according to whether they have a state-level sales tax and a state-level income tax:

- State income tax & state sales tax: 39
- State income tax & no state sales tax: 4
- No state income tax & state sales tax: 6
- No state income tax & no state sales tax: 1

To the nearest tenth of a percent, what percent of states with a state-level sales tax do not have a state-level income tax?
questions.explanation: Condition on having a state sales tax: total with sales tax \(=39+6=45\). Of those, without income tax: 6. Percent \(=\frac{6}{45}=0.1333\ldots\approx 13.3\%\).
choiceExplanations: Uses 6 out of all states (or another wrong denominator).
Likely uses 6 out of 50 (or other incorrect total).
Correct: \(6/45\approx 13.3\%\).
Rounding/denominator error.
